violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was cleaned and sanitized at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Canned peas were not properly reheated before being placed into hot holding. Ready to eat foods taken from a commercially processed can or intact package from a food processing plant must be heated to at least 135 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Peas were observed at 100 DF. Peas were reheated to 147 DF and placed back in the hot hold unit at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Spray bottles were labeled at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

The 3-bay sink was observed in poor repair with a small leak in the 1st bay. The open air unit was observed in poor repair holding an ambient temperature of 45 DF at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold and ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Equipment must be in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025

The floor under the hand sink, and behind the chest freezers has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to properly clean and sanitize effected areas to monitor new activity and is to continue to work with pest control. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment employees a food safety manager, no person in registered with the RIDOH. Owner is to renew RIFSM license with RIDOH by week end. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Corrective Action - Establishment is to continue to work with pest control. Establishment is to continue to clean out back room. Establishment is to continue to clean any and all dropping using proper cleaning and sanitizing methods. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Canned sweet potatoes were only heated to 92 degrees Fahrenheit before being placed into hot holding. Ready to eat foods taken from a commercially processed can or intact package from a food processing plant must be heated to at least 135 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Food was placed in a stove and brought up to 171 Degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. Droppings were observed behind the non food contact surfaces of the cooking line, around the floor in the kitchen, by the grease trap, in the back cook area, by the service stink, and in the back storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Corrective Action - Establishment is to have pest control do a full evalution of premises today. Establishment is to have pest control leave a report for RIDOH. Establishment is to have pest control contact RIDOH today after inspection. Establishment is to clean and sanitize all area after the removal of any and all droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Sanitizer was observed at 250 PPM New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the drying rack have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024
The hood in the back cooking area was observed with an excess of grease built up and dripping onto the floor. Hood systems must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2024
